tags.name: Auth
tags.description:
This section describes the process of authentication and access management for the system's API using JWT tokens, which are passed in the request header under the key access-token.
There are two types of tokens: • Basic access token — used for requests that are not tied to a specific company. • Company-specific access token — used for requests related to a specific company.
Usage Steps Step 1 — Obtaining a Basic Access Token
Send a request to /auth/api-login with the email and password of a user registered specifically for API usage.
The obtained token can be used for requests not tied to a company (e.g., /company/api-readDetailedList to retrieve a list of companies). Step 2 — Retrieving the Company Identifier
Using the basic access token, send a request to /company/api-readDetailedList and find the code or uuid of the required company. Step 3 — Obtaining a Company-Specific Access Token
Pass the basic access token and the company code in a request to /auth/api-selectCompany.
In the response, you will receive companyAccessToken, which should be passed in the request header (access-token) for all operations related to this company (e.g., /invoiceForCustomerPayment/api-readList).

Token Expiration and Renewal • Access token (basic or company-specific) is valid for 1 hour. • Refresh token is valid for 1 week.

Token renewal process: • If the access token has expired, send a request to /auth/api-refresh, providing the valid refresh token. • If the refresh token has also expired, you must re-authenticate via /auth/api-login to obtain a new set of tokens.
